   140→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   141→# (3) Single-turn session (too few turns for compaction)
   142→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   143→
   144→class TestSingleTurn:
   145→    def test_no_eviction_default_threshold(self, tmp_path):
   146→        """One API call with 2 user turns and default age_threshold=4
   147→        produces no evictions (max turns_from_end = 1 < 4)."""
   148→        messages = [
   149→            {"role": "user", "content": "read /foo.py"},
   150→            {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   151→                _tool_use("tu_1", "Read", {"file_path": "/foo.py"})
   152→            ]},
   153→            {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_1", BIG)]},
   154→        ]
   155→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "single.jsonl", [_req(messages)])
   156→        s = replay_session(p)
   157→
   158→        assert s.total_turns == 1
   159→        assert s.total_evictions == 0
   160→        assert s.total_faults == 0
   161→        assert len(s.turns) == 1
   162→        t = s.turns[0]
   163→        assert t.turn == 1
   164→        assert t.evictions == 0
   165→        assert t.faults == 0
   166→        assert t.bytes_saved == 0
   167→        assert t.bytes_original == t.bytes_compacted
   168→
   169→    def test_small_results_never_evicted(self, tmp_path):
   170→        """Even if old enough, results below min_size are kept."""
   171→        # 4 user turns with age_threshold=2: turn 1 has tfend=3 (string, skip),
   172→        # turn 2 has tfend=2 (eligible), but content is SMALL → skipped_small.
   173→        messages = [
   174→            {"role": "user", "content": "read"},
   175→            {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   176→                _tool_use("tu_1", "Read", {"file_path": "/tiny.py"})
   177→            ]},
   178→            {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_1", SMALL)]},
   179→            {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "ok"}]},
   180→            {"role": "user", "content": "next"},
   181→            {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "sure"}]},
   182→            {"role": "user", "content": "done"},
   183→        ]
   184→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "small.jsonl", [_req(messages)])
   185→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   186→        assert s.total_evictions == 0
   187→
   188→    def test_error_results_never_evicted(self, tmp_path):
   189→        """Error tool results are always preserved, regardless of age."""
   190→        messages = [
   191→            {"role": "user", "content": "try"},
   192→            {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   193→                _tool_use("tu_1", "Bash", {"command": "false"})
   194→            ]},
   195→            {"role": "user", "content": [
   196→                _tool_result("tu_1", BIG, is_error=True)
   197→            ]},
   198→            {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "failed"}]},
   199→            {"role": "user", "content": "ok"},
   200→            {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "sure"}]},
   201→            {"role": "user", "content": "done"},
   202→        ]
   203→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "error.jsonl", [_req(messages)])
   204→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   205→        assert s.total_evictions == 0

   208→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   209→# (4) Multi-turn session where tool results age past threshold
   210→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   211→
   212→def _multi_turn_request_1() -> dict:
   213→    """First API call: 4 user turns. With age_threshold=2, the tool_result
   214→    in user turn 2 (tu_1) is old enough to be evicted (turns_from_end=2)."""
   215→    messages = [
   216→        {"role": "user", "content": "read foo"},
   217→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   218→            _tool_use("tu_1", "Read", {"file_path": "/foo.py"})
   219→        ]},
   220→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_1", BIG)]},
   221→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "got it"}]},
   222→        {"role": "user", "content": "read bar"},
   223→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   224→            _tool_use("tu_2", "Read", {"file_path": "/bar.py"})
   225→        ]},
   226→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_2", BIG)]},
   227→    ]
   228→    return _req(messages, ts="2026-03-01T12:00:00Z")
   229→
   230→
   231→def _multi_turn_request_2() -> dict:
   232→    """Second API call: 6 user turns. tu_1 was evicted in request 1.
   233→    Now tu_2 (user turn 4, turns_from_end=2) also gets evicted."""
   234→    messages = [
   235→        {"role": "user", "content": "read foo"},
   236→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   237→            _tool_use("tu_1", "Read", {"file_path": "/foo.py"})
   238→        ]},
   239→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_1", BIG)]},
   240→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "got it"}]},
   241→        {"role": "user", "content": "read bar"},
   242→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   243→            _tool_use("tu_2", "Read", {"file_path": "/bar.py"})
   244→        ]},
   245→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_2", BIG)]},
   246→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "ok"}]},
   247→        {"role": "user", "content": "do stuff"},
   248→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   249→            _tool_use("tu_5", "Grep", {"pattern": "TODO", "path": "."})
   250→        ]},
   251→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_5", BIG)]},
   252→    ]
   253→    return _req(messages, ts="2026-03-01T12:01:00Z")
   254→
   255→
   256→class TestMultiTurnEviction:
   257→    def test_first_request_evicts_oldest(self, tmp_path):
   258→        """Request 1: 4 user turns, threshold=2, tu_1 evicted."""
   259→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "multi.jsonl", [_multi_turn_request_1()])
   260→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   261→
   262→        assert s.total_turns == 1
   263→        assert s.total_evictions == 1
   264→        t = s.turns[0]
   265→        assert t.evictions == 1
   266→        assert t.bytes_saved > 0
   267→
   268→    def test_cumulative_evictions_across_requests(self, tmp_path):
   269→        """Two requests: tu_1 evicted in req 1, tu_2 evicted in req 2."""
   270→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _multi_turn_request_2()]
   271→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "multi2.jsonl", records)
   272→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   273→
   274→        assert s.total_turns == 2
   275→        assert s.total_evictions == 2
   276→        assert s.turns[0].evictions == 1
   277→        assert s.turns[0].cumulative_evictions == 1
   278→        assert s.turns[1].cumulative_evictions == 2

   280→    def test_bytes_saved_increases(self, tmp_path):
   281→        """Total bytes saved grows with each eviction."""

   302→    def test_apply_evictions_replaces_previously_evicted(self, tmp_path):

   342→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   343→# (5) Page fault detection when model re-requests evicted content
   344→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   345→
   346→def _fault_request_2() -> dict:
   347→    """Second request that includes a re-read of /foo.py (tu_3) after
   348→    tu_1 (Read /foo.py) was evicted in request 1. tu_3 is a page fault."""
   349→    messages = [
   350→        {"role": "user", "content": "read foo"},
   351→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   352→            _tool_use("tu_1", "Read", {"file_path": "/foo.py"})
   353→        ]},
   354→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_1", BIG)]},
   355→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "got it"}]},
   356→        {"role": "user", "content": "read bar"},
   357→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   358→            _tool_use("tu_2", "Read", {"file_path": "/bar.py"})
   359→        ]},
   360→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_2", BIG)]},
   361→        # The model re-requests /foo.py (page fault)
   362→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   363→            _tool_use("tu_3", "Read", {"file_path": "/foo.py"})
   364→        ]},
   365→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_3", "re-read content")]},
   366→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "done"}]},
   367→        {"role": "user", "content": "continue"},
   368→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   369→            _tool_use("tu_6", "Grep", {"pattern": "TODO", "path": "."})
   370→        ]},
   371→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_6", BIG)]},
   372→    ]
   373→    return _req(messages, ts="2026-03-01T12:01:00Z")
   374→
   375→
   376→class TestPageFaultDetection:
   377→    def test_fault_detected(self, tmp_path):
   378→        """A re-read of /foo.py after tu_1 eviction is a page fault."""
   379→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _fault_request_2()]
   380→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "fault.jsonl", records)
   381→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   382→
   383→        # Request 1: tu_1 evicted, no faults.
   384→        assert s.turns[0].faults == 0
   385→        # Request 2: tu_3 re-reads /foo.py → fault detected.
   386→        assert s.turns[1].faults == 1
   387→        assert s.total_faults == 1
   388→
   389→    def test_fault_rate(self, tmp_path):
   390→        """fault_rate = total_faults / total_evictions."""
   391→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _fault_request_2()]
   392→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "faultrate.jsonl", records)
   393→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   394→
   395→        assert s.total_evictions > 0
   396→        expected = s.total_faults / s.total_evictions
   397→        assert abs(s.fault_rate - expected) < 1e-9
   398→
   399→    def test_cumulative_faults(self, tmp_path):
   400→        """cumulative_faults tracks running total across turns."""
   401→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _fault_request_2()]
   402→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "cumfault.jsonl", records)
   403→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   404→
   405→        assert s.turns[0].cumulative_faults == 0
   406→        assert s.turns[1].cumulative_faults == 1
   407→
   408→    def test_original_tool_use_not_counted_as_fault(self, tmp_path):
   409→        """The original tool_use (tu_1) that produced the evicted result
   410→        should NOT be counted as a fault even though it matches."""
   411→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1()]
   412→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "nofault.jsonl", records)
   413→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   414→
   415→        # tu_1 is in the assistant message AND was evicted, but it's the
   416→        # original call — not a re-request. No fault.
   417→        assert s.total_faults == 0
   418→
   419→    def test_no_double_counting_across_requests(self, tmp_path):
   420→        """The same fault tool_use_id appearing in multiple request records
   421→        should only be counted once."""
   422→        req1 = _multi_turn_request_1()
   423→        req2 = _fault_request_2()
   424→        # Request 3 is a superset of request 2 (session has grown).
   425→        # tu_3 (the fault) is still in the messages.
   426→        msgs3 = json.loads(json.dumps(req2["messages_full"]))
   427→        msgs3.extend([
   428→            {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "more"}]},
   429→            {"role": "user", "content": "keep going"},
   430→        ])
   431→        req3 = _req(msgs3, ts="2026-03-01T12:02:00Z")
   432→
   433→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "dedup.jsonl", [req1, req2, req3])
   434→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   435→
   436→        # tu_3 appears in both req2 and req3, but only counted once.
   437→        assert s.total_faults == 1
   438→

   665→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   666→# (8) Aggregate stats across multiple sessions
   667→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   668→
   669→class TestAggregateStats:
   670→    def _make_two_sessions(self, tmp_path):
   671→        """Create two log files with known eviction/fault patterns."""
   672→        # Session A: 1 eviction, 0 faults
   673→        p_a = _write_jsonl(
   674→            tmp_path, "a.jsonl", [_multi_turn_request_1()],
   675→        )
   676→        # Session B: 2 evictions, 1 fault (re-read of /foo.py)
   677→        p_b = _write_jsonl(
   678→            tmp_path, "b.jsonl",
   679→            [_multi_turn_request_1(), _fault_request_2()],
   680→        )
   681→        return p_a, p_b
   682→
   683→    def test_aggregate_totals(self, tmp_path):
   684→        """Aggregate totals equal the sum of per-session values."""
   685→        p_a, p_b = self._make_two_sessions(tmp_path)
   686→        s_a = replay_session(p_a, age_threshold=2)
   687→        s_b = replay_session(p_b, age_threshold=2)
   688→        sessions = [s_a, s_b]
   689→
   690→        total_turns = sum(s.total_turns for s in sessions)
   691→        total_evictions = sum(s.total_evictions for s in sessions)
   692→        total_bytes_saved = sum(s.total_bytes_saved for s in sessions)
   693→        total_bytes_original = sum(s.total_bytes_original for s in sessions)
   694→        total_faults = sum(s.total_faults for s in sessions)
   695→
   696→        assert total_turns == s_a.total_turns + s_b.total_turns
   697→        assert total_evictions == s_a.total_evictions + s_b.total_evictions
   698→        assert total_bytes_saved == s_a.total_bytes_saved + s_b.total_bytes_saved
   699→        assert total_bytes_original == (
   700→            s_a.total_bytes_original + s_b.total_bytes_original
   701→        )
   702→        assert total_faults == s_a.total_faults + s_b.total_faults
   703→
   704→    def test_aggregate_fault_rate(self, tmp_path):
   705→        """Aggregate fault_rate = total_faults / total_evictions."""
   706→        p_a, p_b = self._make_two_sessions(tmp_path)
   707→        s_a = replay_session(p_a, age_threshold=2)
   708→        s_b = replay_session(p_b, age_threshold=2)
   709→
   710→        total_e = s_a.total_evictions + s_b.total_evictions
   711→        total_f = s_a.total_faults + s_b.total_faults
   712→        expected_rate = total_f / total_e if total_e > 0 else 0.0
   713→
   714→        assert total_e > 0
   715→        assert abs(expected_rate - total_f / total_e) < 1e-9
   716→
   717→    def test_aggregate_json_output(self, tmp_path, capsys, monkeypatch):
   718→        """--json with multiple logs produces per-session + aggregate lines."""
   719→        p_a, p_b = self._make_two_sessions(tmp_path)
   720→
   721→        monkeypatch.setattr(
   722→            sys, "argv",
   723→            ["replay", "--json", "--age-threshold", "2", str(p_a), str(p_b)],
   724→        )
   725→        main()
   726→
   727→        lines = capsys.readouterr().out.strip().split("\n")
   728→        assert len(lines) == 3
   729→
   730→        sa = json.loads(lines[0])
   731→        sb = json.loads(lines[1])
   732→        agg = json.loads(lines[2])
   733→
   734→        assert agg["type"] == "aggregate"
   735→        assert agg["sessions"] == 2
   736→        assert agg["total_evictions"] == sa["total_evictions"] + sb["total_evictions"]
   737→        assert agg["total_bytes_saved"] == (
   738→            sa["total_bytes_saved"] + sb["total_bytes_saved"]
   739→        )
   740→        assert agg["total_faults"] == sa["total_faults"] + sb["total_faults"]
   741→        assert agg["total_bytes_original"] == (

     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import json
     6→import sys

     8→from pathlib import Path
     9→
    10→import pytest

    12→from pichay.trimmer import (
    13→    SessionTrimReport,
    14→    SkillDedupeStats,
    15→    StaticCacheStats,
    16→    SystemPromptTrimmer,
    17→    ToolStubStats,
    18→    TrimResult,
    19→    TrimTurn,
    20→    _STUB_SCHEMA,

    22→    _dedupe_skills_text,

    30→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31→# Helpers
    32→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    33→
    34→def _write_jsonl(tmp_path: Path, name: str, records: list[dict]) -> Path:
    35→    """Write a list of dicts as JSONL and return the path."""
    36→    p = tmp_path / name
    37→    with open(p, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    38→        for rec in records:
    39→            f.write(json.dumps(rec) + "\n")
    40→    return p
    41→
    42→
    43→def _tool_def(name: str, desc: str = "A tool.", schema: dict | None = None) -> dict:
    44→    """Create a tool definition dict."""
    45→    return {
    46→        "name": name,
    47→        "description": desc,
    48→        "input_schema": schema or {
    49→            "type": "object",
    50→            "properties": {"arg": {"type": "string"}},
    51→            "required": ["arg"],
    52→        },
    53→    }
    54→
    55→
    56→def _tool_use(tid: str, name: str, inp: dict | None = None) -> dict:
    57→    return {"type": "tool_use", "id": tid, "name": name, "input": inp or {}}
    58→
    59→
    60→def _tool_result(tid: str, content: str = "ok") -> dict:
    61→    return {"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": tid, "content": content}
    62→
    63→
    64→def _skill_reminder(entries: list[str]) -> str:
    65→    """Build a <system-reminder> block with a skill list."""
    66→    lines = "\n".join(f"- {e}" for e in entries)
    67→    return (
    68→        "<system-reminder>\n"
    69→        "The following skills are available for use with the Skill tool:\n\n"
    70→        f"{lines}\n"
    71→        "</system-reminder>"
    72→    )
    73→
    74→
    75→def _body(
    76→    tools: list[dict] | None = None,
    77→    messages: list[dict] | None = None,
    78→    system: list[dict] | str | None = None,
    79→) -> dict:
    80→    """Build a minimal request body."""
    81→    b: dict = {}
    82→    if tools is not None:
    83→        b["tools"] = tools
    84→    if messages is not None:
    85→        b["messages"] = messages
    86→    else:
    87→        b["messages"] = []
    88→    if system is not None:
    89→        b["system"] = system
    90→    return b
    91→

    36→    36→def _write_jsonl(tmp_path: Path, name: str, records: list[dict]) -> Path:
    37→    37→    """Write a list of dicts as JSONL and return the path."""
    38→    38→    p = tmp_path / name
    39→    39→    with open(p, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    40→    40→        for rec in records:
    41→    41→            f.write(json.dumps(rec) + "\n")
    42→    42→    return p

# System
 - All text you output outside of tool use is displayed to the user. Output text to communicate with the user. You can use Github-flavored markdown for formatting, and will be rendered in a monospace font using the CommonMark specification.

When you attempt to call a tool that is not automatically allowed by the user's permission mode or permission settings, the user will be prompted so that they can approve or deny the execution. If the user denies a tool you call, do not re-attempt the exact same tool call. Instead, think about why the user has denied the tool call and adjust your approach. If you do not understand why the user has denied a tool call, use the AskUserQuestion to ask them.

Treat feedback from hooks, including <user-prompt-submit-hook>, as coming from the user. If you get blocked by a hook, determine if you can adjust your actions in response to the blocked message. If not, ask the user to check their hooks configuration.

 - The user will primarily request you to perform software engineering tasks. These may include solving bugs, adding new functionality, refactoring code, explaining code, and more. When given an unclear or generic instruction, consider it in the context of these software engineering tasks and the current working directory. For example, if the user asks you to change "methodName" to snake case, do not reply with just "method_name", instead find the method in the code and modify the code.
 - You are highly capable and often allow users to complete ambitious tasks that would otherwise be too complex or take too long. You should defer to user judgement about whether a task is too large to attempt.
 - In general, do not propose changes to code you haven't read. If a user asks about or wants you to modify a file, read it first. Understand existing code before suggesting modifications.

Instead, consider alternative approaches or other ways you might unblock yourself, or consider using the AskUserQuestion to align with the user on the right path forward.
 - Be careful not to introduce security vulnerabilities such as command injection, XSS, SQL injection, and other OWASP top 10 vulnerabilities. If you notice that you wrote insecure code, immediately fix it. Prioritize writing safe, secure, and correct code.
 - Avoid over-engineering. Only make changes that are directly requested or clearly necessary. Keep solutions simple and focused.
  - Don't add features, refactor code, or make "improvements" beyond what was asked. A bug fix doesn't need surrounding code cleaned up. A simple feature doesn't need extra configurability. Don't add docstrings, comments, or type annotations to code you didn't change. Only add comments where the logic isn't self-evident.
  - Don't add error handling, fallbacks, or validation for scenarios that can't happen. Trust internal code and framework guarantees. Only validate at system boundaries (user input, external APIs). Don't use feature flags or backwards-compatibility shims when you can just change the code.

  - /help: Get help with using Claude Code
  - To give feedback, users should report the issue at https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues

# Executing actions with care

Carefully consider the reversibility and blast radius of actions. Generally you can freely take local, reversible actions like editing files or running tests. But for actions that are hard to reverse, affect shared systems beyond your local environment, or could otherwise be risky or destructive, check with the user before proceeding. The cost of pausing to confirm is low, while the cost of an unwanted action (lost work, unintended messages sent, deleted branches) can be very high. For actions like these, consider the context, the action, and user instructions, and by default transparently communicate the action and ask for confirmation before proceeding. This default can be changed by user instructions - if explicitly asked to operate more autonomously, then you may proceed without confirmation, but still attend to the risks and consequences when taking actions.
